description:
## Change

In `ir_codegen.rs`:
- `:3958` (resolve_cont_sid): drop the `panic!`; rely on the `resolve` total
  guarantee from fz-aiz.4.
- `:4005` (resolve_callee_sid_in): same.

The `.unwrap_or_else(...)` becomes `.expect("...")` with a tight invariant
message, or — since `resolve` is now total — simply `.unwrap()` with a
debug-only assertion documenting the invariant.

## Integration test: quicksort fixture

Add `fixtures/quicksort/` exercising the HOF + non-tail-recursion + closures
shape that surfaced the bugs:

```fz
fn filter(_, []), do: []
fn filter(pred, [h | t]) do
  if pred(h) do [h | filter(pred, t)] else filter(pred, t) end
end

fn concat([], ys), do: ys
fn concat([h | t], ys), do: [h | concat(t, ys)]

fn quicksort([]), do: []
fn quicksort([p | t]) do
  less    = filter(fn(x) -> x < p, t)
  greater = filter(fn(x) -> x >= p, t)
  concat(quicksort(less), [p | quicksort(greater)])
end

fn main() do
  print(quicksort([]))
  print(quicksort([42]))
  print(quicksort([5, 4, 3, 2, 1]))
  print(quicksort([3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6, 5, 3, 5]))
end
```

`paths: [jit, interp, aot]`. This shape only compiles cleanly after fz-aiz.1
(if/else multi-line) and fz-aiz.4 (resolve total). It's THE proof that the
arc works.

## Tests

- Quicksort fixture passes on `jit`, `interp` (if not blocked on Discriminant), `aot`.
- Existing `expected.specs` goldens may shift — bless and review.
- Existing `expected.clif` goldens should be stable (codegen body changes
  are limited to the dispatch lookup, not body emission).

## Blocked by fz-aiz.4 (and effectively fz-aiz.1 + fz-aiz.2 for the fixture
shape to parse and not warn-as-error). Blocks fz-aiz.6.
title: fz-aiz.5: replace .29.11 / .29.12.1 panics with degraded dispatch; quicksort integration fixture
